packet.hypothesis_text: If a candidate discovery claim names measurable variables, boundary conditions, and a falsification route, then downstream reviewers can separate test-ready hypotheses from narrative-only explanations.
missingness: No real reviewer labels attached yet
packet.revision_reason: Convert generated reasoning into a gateable verification packet before memory admission.

relations: claims with variables and falsification routes are more reviewable than claims without them
mechanistic_model.expected_direction: More complete verification fields should increase admission confidence.
boundary_conditions: No diagnosis, treatment, or private identifiers

measurable_variables.name: variable_completion_rate
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Fraction of required measurable-variable fields completed with non-empty operational definitions.

measurable_variables.name: boundary_violation_count
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Number of private, advice-like, or overclaiming phrases detected in the packet.

packet.falsification_route: The bridge fails if packets lacking measurement definitions, privacy boundaries, or falsification routes are admitted as test-ready.
packet.next_executable_action: Run validator against synthetic pass/fail fixtures and reject non-compliant packets.

packet.hypothesis_text: This claim contains a private case-specific assertion and should not enter public discovery memory.
packet.revision_reason: Reject private content before public memory admission.

relations: private observations cannot be admitted into public artifacts

measurable_variables.name: private_marker_count
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Count of private identifiers or specific case details.

measurable_variables.name: advice_leak_count
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Count of treatment or diagnosis recommendations.

packet.falsification_route: The validator is falsified if reject_private packets are admitted to hypothesis backlog.
packet.next_executable_action: Reject the packet and return a privacy-boundary error.

packet.hypothesis_text: This generated hypothesis is already supported even though no test result, dataset, or reproducible evidence is attached.

mechanistic_model.expected_direction: Evidence-free support claims should be rejected.
boundary_conditions: No support without a test result

measurable_variables.name: evidence_strength_rank
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Ordinal strength of evidence attached to the packet.

packet.falsification_route: The bridge is falsified if a supported claim with evidence_strength none passes admission.
packet.next_executable_action: Route packet back to missingness queue.

packet.hypothesis_text: A hypothesis can remain useful if it is explicitly held for missingness instead of being deleted or promoted beyond available evidence.
packet.revision_reason: Preserve incomplete but bounded hypotheses without overclaiming.

mechanistic_model.expected_direction: Bounded missingness should improve reviewability compared with silent gaps.

measurable_variables.name: missingness_item_count
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Number of explicit missingness items listed.

measurable_variables.name: next_action_specificity
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Whether the next action names an executable artifact or validation step.

packet.falsification_route: The bridge fails if incomplete candidates cannot be preserved without being falsely promoted or erased.
packet.next_executable_action: Add reviewer-label fixtures and retest admission decision stability.
